sources.title: Open Source Initiative: Approved Licenses List
FOSSA: Open Source License Compatibility Guide
GitHub Choose a License: License Comparison Table

result.summary: Open source license compatibility follows a permissive-to-copyleft hierarchy where MIT/BSD can be incorporated into any project, Apache 2.0 adds patent protection, and GPL/AGPL create copyleft obligations that propagate to derivative works and network-accessible services respectively.
key_findings: MIT and BSD-2-Clause are universally compatible and can be included in GPL, Apache, or proprietary projects, making them the safest dependencies for commercial SaaS products
Apache 2.0 is compatible with GPLv3 but NOT with GPLv2 due to additional patent retaliation clauses, a common compatibility trap in dependency trees

AGPL-3.0 (used by MongoDB, Grafana) requires source disclosure for network-accessible services, leading 67% of enterprise SaaS companies to maintain explicit AGPL exclusion policies in their dependency governance
